converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Bernhard Eduard Fernow papers
Fernow, B. E. (Bernhard Eduard), 1851-1923.
20-1-561
2.7 cubic feet. 2.7 cubic feet.
1885-1930. Correspondence, Manuscripts, Newspapers, Publications, Scrapbooks Includes manuscripts, speeches and articles by Fernow concerning forestry. Collection material in English 3990952
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
Date completed:
August 2008
EAD encoding:
RMC Staff, August 2008
Date modified:
Kristen Reichenbach, January 2018
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Bernhard Fernow was Chief of the Division of Forestry, U.S. Department of Agriculture (1886-1898), and Director of the New York State College of Forestry at Cornell University (1898-1903).
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Includes manuscripts of speeches and articles by Fernow concerning forestry, forest legislation, and the Division of Forestry, U.S. Department of Agriculture; letters concerning the history of forestry and forest legislation in the United States; correspondence related to Fernow's position at Cornell, with J. Sterling Morton, U.S. Secretary of Agriculture, with commercial firms in the United States and Germany, and with the New York State Forest, Fish and Game Commission; scrapbooks of clippings concerning forestry, the College of Forestry at Cornell, artificial rain making, and Fernow himself; corrected printer's proof of his book ECONOMICS OF FORESTRY; and family correspondence. Also includes correspondence between Fernow and Captain Ahern, head of the Philippine forestry service, concerning the recruitment of foresters to serve under Ahern, and including remarks on the alleged mismanagement of Philippine forests by the Spanish, 1900-1902.
Cite As:
Bernhard Eduard Fernow papers, #20-1-561. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Conditions Governing Use
Box 3, folder 13 contains a broken glass plate negative contained within an envelope. Users should take precautions when handling.
Foresters. Germany. Philippines. Rain-making. Forests and forestry -- Philippines. Forests and forestry -- History. Forestry law and legislation. Morton, J. Sterling. Ahern, Captain. Fernow, Bernhard Eduard. (Title of work: Economics of forestry..) Cornell University -- : Faculty. New York State College of Forestry New York (State). Forest, Fish and Game Commission United States. Department of Agriculture. Division of Forestry

Scope and Contents
Includes one report addressed to the Adirondack League Club re: cutting on Club land and forest policyVarious addresses and papers1885-1900226
Scope and Contents
Includes one speech on the trees of Buffalo, conservation, forest management and "What interest has the farmers in forestry and the Adirondack problem"Various addresses and papers on forestry policy, forestry propaganda, and farmers and forestry1885-1900227Various addresses and papers on forestry in Russia and Alaska, the public and forestry, forest reservations1885-1900228
Scope and Contents
Also includes drafts for "The Forest as a Condition"Correspondence with the New York Botanical Garden regarding Cuba Hardwood Company1906229Various publications and reports on U.S. forestry policy1920230Report on Township 261898231University of Toronto Faculty of Forestry correspondence1914-1920232Newspaper clippings1914-192031Department of Agriculture, Forestry Division correspondence predominantly with Office of the Secretary1887-189532Correspondence with German colleagues1886-1915333 photos of Axton forest, article in "Cornell Daily Sun" re: Fernow and "Journal of Forestry" containing article re: Fernow1902-192336Articles mainly regarding Fernow1897-192137Letters from brother-in-law John Reynolds1886-189538Letters from William Bruce King1914-191539Letters from sons Edward and Fritz, and daughter Rose1909-1911310Records re: naturalization1877-1883311Correspondence re: Axton Forest1919-1921312The Intimate History of the Rise and Fall of the New York College of Forestry at Cornell UniversityDate Unknown313
Conditions Governing Use
Contains a broken glass plate negative contained within an envelope. Users should take precautions when handling.Correspondence regarding closing of College of Forestry1903314Correspondence with tree nurseries regarding the purchase of seeds and seedlings for the CollegeFeb/Mar '94315Record of planting and nursery work at the Axton Forest1899-1900316Comments on Axton Plantations by Dr. Fernow, records of plantings and observations of Axton Sample Plots1919-1932317"History of the Constitutional Amendment (Article VII, section 7) of the College Forest Experiment," by Dr. Fernow, notes and reports on the Axton Forest1900-1919318Map of Cornell College Forest and correspondence re: Axton plantations1919-1933319Correspondence, plot surveys, and one map regarding Axton Forest1906-1932320Economics of ForestryDate unknown3
Scope and Contents
Three volumes.Letter from Gifforrd Pinchot, first chief of the US Forestry Service to FernowDate unknown3 ======= Alphabetical Correspondence1895-1921General correspondence A-N1895-192134Correspondence - Bu1898-190218Correspondence - Ca - Ci1898-190219Correspondence - Ch - Cl1898-1902110Correspondence - Co, excluding Collins1898-1902111Correspondence - letters to Thomas W.
